reasoning: `container_map_4k` tracks per-container page ownership but `wf()` does not require these pages to be in `allocated_pages_4k`. The preconditions are simultaneously satisfiable — a container can claim a page that is free or in any other state, breaking the containment invariant.
Same pattern as 4k — `wf()` never constrains `container_map_2m` entries to be a subset of `allocated_pages_2m`. Containers can reference non-allocated 2M pages.
Same pattern — `container_map_1g` is unconstrained relative to `allocated_pages_1g` in `wf()`. Containers can reference non-allocated 1G pages.

The `free_pages_4k_wf` ensures free pages have state `Free4k`, and `allocated_pages_4k_wf` ensures allocated pages have state `Allocated4k`. Since each page has exactly one state in `page_array`, a page cannot simultaneously satisfy both `Free4k` and `Allocated4k`. The preconditions are unsatisfiable under `wf()`.

`mapped_pages_4k_wf` only links the mapped set to `PageState::Mapped4k` in the page array — it never requires `mappings@` to be non-empty. A page can transition to Mapped4k state and remain there even after all its virtual address mappings are removed, creating a stale "mapped" page with no actual mappings.
